info.title: Billing API
info.description:
API to manage billing data for epilot contracts and orders.

This API provides endpoints for managing financial transactions (Buchungssätze) related to
customer contracts (Verträge) including installments (Abschlagszahlungen), payments (Zahlungseingänge),
reimbursements (Rückerstattungen), and other billing events.

## Key Concepts

- **Billing Event (Buchungssatz)**: A single financial transaction entry in the billing ledger
- **Contract (Vertrag)**: A customer agreement linked to billing events
- **Balance (Kontostand)**: The current financial standing of a customer across all contracts

tags.name: Billing Events
tags.description:
Manage billing events (Buchungssätze) such as installments (Abschlagszahlungen),
payments (Zahlungseingänge), reimbursements (Rückerstattungen), dunning fees (Mahngebühren),
and other financial transactions.

Billing events follow double-entry accounting principles where each entry is either
a debit (Soll) or credit (Haben) transaction.

tags.name: Contracts
tags.description:
Manage contract entities (Verträge) within epilot 360.

Contracts represent customer agreements and serve as the parent entity for billing events.
Each contract has associated billing settings such as billing period (Abrechnungszeitraum),
installment amounts (Abschlagsbeträge), and balance tracking.

tags.name: Balance
tags.description:
Retrieve customer balance information (Kontostand).

The balance represents the total financial standing of a customer across all their
contracts and orders, calculated from the sum of all billing events.

tags.name: Pricing Information
tags.description: Read contract and billing account pricing information

get.description:
Retrieve and filter billing events (Buchungssätze) such as installments (Abschlagszahlungen),
payments (Zahlungseingänge), and reimbursements (Rückerstattungen).

Results are paginated and can be filtered by entity, contact, event type, or date range.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Pagination offset - number of results to skip

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Filter billing events by one or more entity IDs (e.g., contract or order IDs)

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Filter billing events by customer contact ID (Kundennummer)

parameters.name: event_type
parameters.description:
Filter by billing event type (Buchungsart):
- `installment`: Abschlagszahlung (scheduled payment due)
- `reimbursement`: Rückerstattung (refund to customer)

parameters.name: date_after
parameters.description: Filter billing events with booking date (Buchungsdatum) after this timestamp

parameters.name: date_before
parameters.description: Filter billing events with booking date (Buchungsdatum) before this timestamp

200.description: Paginated list of billing events matching the filter criteria

hits.description: Total number of billing events matching the query

results.description: List of billing events for the current page

post.description:
Create a new billing event (Buchungssatz) such as an installment (Abschlagszahlung),
payment (Zahlungseingang), or reimbursement (Rückerstattung).

The billing event will be linked to a contract and will affect the customer's balance.

get.description: Retrieve a single billing event (Buchungssatz) by its unique ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the billing event (Buchungssatz-ID)

patch.description:
Update an existing billing event (Buchungssatz).

Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated.
Common use cases include updating status from "open" to "closed" after payment,
or correcting billing amounts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the billing event to update

requestBody.description: Fields to update on the billing event

delete.description:
Delete an existing billing event (Buchungssatz).

**Warning**: Deleting billing events affects the customer's balance calculation.
Consider using a correction event (Korrekturbuchung) instead for audit purposes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the billing event to delete

get.description:
Retrieve a billing event (Buchungssatz) by its external system identifier.

This endpoint is useful for integrations with external systems such as SAP FI/CA,
ERP systems, or payment processors that maintain their own reference IDs.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description:
External system identifier for the billing event.
For example, a SAP document number or payment processor reference ID.

post.description:
Create a new contract entity (Vertrag) for billing purposes.

Contracts serve as the parent entity for billing events and contain billing
settings such as installment amounts (Abschlagsbeträge), billing periods
(Abrechnungszeiträume), and customer addresses.

patch.description:
Update an existing contract entity (Vertrag).

Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated.
Common use cases include updating billing settings, changing status,
or recording termination details.

requestBody.description: Fields to update on the contract

delete.description:
Delete an existing contract entity (Vertrag).

**Warning**: Deleting a contract will affect all associated billing events.
Consider setting the contract status to "terminated" instead for audit purposes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the contract to delete

get.description: Get current pricing information and recent configuration history for a Contract.
Get current pricing information for the active Contracts linked to a Billing Account.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the Billing Account entity

get.description: Get billing configuration history for a Contract.
Get merged billing configuration history for active Contracts linked to a Billing Account.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the Billing Account entity

get.description:
Retrieve the total balance (Kontostand) across all contracts and orders for a customer.

The balance is calculated from the sum of all billing events (Buchungssätze) associated
with the customer's contracts. A positive balance indicates the customer owes money;
a negative balance indicates a credit (Guthaben) in the customer's favor.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Customer entity ID. This can be either a contact ID (Kontakt-ID) or
an account ID (Kundenkonto-ID).

Size.name: size
Size.description: Maximum number of results to return.

ConfigurationHistoryChangeType.name: change_type
ConfigurationHistoryChangeType.description: Billing configuration change type to return.

BadRequest.description:
Bad Request - The request was malformed or contains invalid parameters.
Check the request body and query parameters for syntax errors.

example.message: Invalid date format for 'due_date'. Expected ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD).

Unauthorized.description:
Unauthorized - Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.
Ensure a valid Bearer token is included in the Authorization header.

Forbidden.description:
Forbidden - The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this action.
Contact your organization administrator to request access.

example.message: You do not have permission to access this resource.

NotFound.description:
Not Found - The requested resource does not exist or has been deleted.
Verify the ID is correct and the resource exists in your organization.

example.message: Billing event with ID '5da0a718-c822-403d-9f5d-20d4584e0528' not found.

UnprocessableEntity.description:
Unprocessable Entity - The request was well-formed but contains semantic errors.
This typically indicates validation failures such as missing required fields
or invalid field values.

example.message: Validation failed: 'billing_amount' must be a positive integer.

InternalServerError.description:
Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred on the server.
Please try again later or contact support if the issue persists.

example.message: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

error.description: Error type or HTTP status text

BaseEntity.description: Base schema for all epilot entities with common system fields

_org.description: Organization ID the entity belongs to (Organisations-ID)

_created_at.description: Timestamp when the entity was created (Erstellungszeitpunkt)

_updated_at.description: Timestamp when the entity was last updated (Aktualisierungszeitpunkt)

EntitySlug.description: URL-friendly identifier for the entity schema (Schema-Slug)

entity_id.description: Entity ID for the related entity (e.g., contract or order)

BaseBillingEvent.description:
Represents a single financial transaction entry (Buchungssatz) in the billing ledger.
Each entry is either a debit or a credit, following double-entry accounting principles.
Common types include Abschlagszahlung (installment), Zahlungseingang (payment), Rückerstattung (reimbursement), etc.

type.description:
The classification of the billing transaction.
This field is used to group financial events for reporting or reconciliation.
Common examples:
  - installment (Abschlagszahlung)
  - payment (Zahlungseingang)
  - reimbursement (Rückerstattung)
  - dunning_fee (Mahngebühr)
  - chargeback (Lastschrift-Rückgabe)
  - final_bill (Endabrechnung)
  - bonus (Gutschrift)
  - correction (Korrekturbuchung)

direction.description:
The accounting direction of the transaction:
- debit (Soll): increases the customer’s liability (e.g. invoice issued)
- credit (Haben): reduces the liability (e.g. payment received)

Automatically inferred based on event type if not specified.

billing_amount.description: Betrag in Cent (net or gross depending on context)

billing_amount_decimal.description: Decimal representation of billing_amount for display or reporting

external_id.description: External system identifier (e.g. SAP FI/CA document number, Zahlungsavis ID)

contract.description: Link to the associated Vertragskonto or contract object

booking_date.description:
General ledger booking date (Buchungsdatum) for the billing event.
Used to determine when the entry was accounted for in the system.
Applies to all event types (debits and credits).
Can be used together with due_date or paid_date

due_date.description: Due date of the invoice or charge (Fälligkeitsdatum)

status.description: Status of the billing event. Defaults to closed.

related_event.description: Optional reference to a previous event e.g. a chargeback or a correction to a previous installment

external_link.description: Optional link to an external resource e.g. an invoice in a customer portal

attachments.description: Optional reference to e.g. an invoice file associated with the billing event

note.description: Öffentliche Notiz sichtbar für den Kunden, z.B. auf der Rechnung oder im Kundenportal

internal_note.description: Interne Notiz, nur sichtbar für Sachbearbeiter (nicht für Kunden sichtbar)
internal_note.example: Rückmeldung von SAP: Betrag aus Zahlungsavis 2025-06-14 übernommen

BillingEvent.description:
Collection of supported billing event types (Buchungsarten).
Each type represents a different kind of financial transaction
that affects the customer's balance.

BillingEventUpdate.description: Fields to update on an existing billing event.

contract.description: Updated link to the associated contract object.

related_event.description: Updated reference to a previous event.

external_link.description: Updated link to an external resource.

attachments.description: Updated invoice file relations associated with the billing event.

note.description: Updated public note visible to the customer.

internal_note.description: Updated internal note visible to agents only.
internal_note.example: Rückmeldung von SAP: Betrag aus Zahlungsavis 2025-06-14 übernommen

InstallmentEvent.description:
Installment billing event (Abschlagszahlung).
Represents a scheduled partial payment that the customer owes,
typically billed monthly for utilities like electricity or gas.

PaymentEvent.description:
Payment received event (Zahlungseingang).
Represents money received from the customer, reducing their balance.
This is a credit transaction.

ReimbursementEvent.description:
Reimbursement event (Rückerstattung).
Represents a refund to the customer, typically after overpayment
or billing correction. This is a credit transaction.

DunningFeeEvent.description:
Dunning fee event (Mahngebühr).
Represents a late payment fee charged to the customer
after a payment reminder has been sent. This is a debit transaction.

FinalBillEvent.description:
Final bill event (Endabrechnung/Schlussrechnung).
Represents the final settlement when a contract ends,
accounting for actual consumption vs. paid installments.
Can be either debit (customer owes more) or credit (customer overpaid).

BonusEvent.description:
Bonus/credit event (Gutschrift/Bonus).
Represents a promotional credit or bonus applied to the customer's account,
such as welcome bonuses or loyalty rewards. This is a credit transaction.

CorrectionEvent.description:
Correction event (Korrekturbuchung).
Represents an adjustment to a previous billing entry,
such as correcting an overcharge or undercharge.
Can be either debit or credit depending on the correction.

CustomEvent.description:
Custom billing event (Benutzerdefinierte Buchung).
Allows for organization-specific billing event types not covered
by the standard types. Use a descriptive type name.

type.description:
Custom billing event type (Freitext).
Use a descriptive identifier for your custom event type.
Examples: grid_fee_adjustment, meter_rental, special_charge

Contract.description:
Represents a customer contract (Vertrag) for billing purposes.
Contracts are the parent entities for billing events and contain
billing configuration such as installment amounts and billing cycles.

status.description:
Current status of the contract (Vertragsstatus):
- draft (Entwurf): Contract is being prepared
- in_approval_process (In Prüfung): Awaiting approval
- approved (Genehmigt): Approved but not yet active
- active (Aktiv): Contract is currently active
- deactivated (Deaktiviert): Temporarily suspended
- revoked (Widerrufen): Cancelled by customer within cooling-off period
- terminated (Gekündigt): Contract has been terminated
- expired (Abgelaufen): Contract term has ended

description.description: Brief description of the contract terms (Vertragsbeschreibung)

branch.description:
Utility branch/commodity type (Sparte):
- power (Strom)
- gas (Gas)
- water (Wasser)
- waste_water (Abwasser)
- district_heating (Fernwärme)

additional_addresses.description: Additional addresses associated with the contract (Weitere Adressen)

termination_date.description: Date when the contract was/will be terminated (Kündigungsdatum)

billing_period.description:
Billing cycle frequency (Abrechnungszeitraum):
- weekly (Wöchentlich)
- monthly (Monatlich)
- every_quarter (Vierteljährlich)
- every_6_months (Halbjährlich)
- yearly (Jährlich)

billing_duration_amount.description: Duration amount for billing period calculation (Abrechnungsdauer)

notice_time_amount.description: The amount of notice required for termination of the contract.

billing_due_day.description: Day of the month when installments are due (Fälligkeitstag). 0 means no fixed billing day is configured.

installment_amount.description:
Fixed installment amount in cents (Abschlagsbetrag in Cent).
Uses integer representation with 2 decimal precision.
Example: 10050 = 100.50 EUR

balance.description:
Current contract balance in cents (Kontostand in Cent).
Positive value = customer owes money (Forderung).
Negative value = customer has credit (Guthaben).

ContractItem.description: Contract entity with all required system fields populated

amount.description: Amount in cents when available or derivable.

amount_decimal.description: Decimal amount string when available or derivable.

billing_due_day.description: Day of the month when installments are due. 0 means no fixed billing day is configured.

Balance.description:
Customer balance summary (Kontostandübersicht).
Represents the aggregated balance across all contracts and orders for a customer.

balance.description:
Total customer balance in cents (Gesamtkontostand in Cent).
Positive = customer owes money (Offener Betrag).
Negative = customer has credit (Guthaben).

balance_decimal.description:
Balance as decimal string for display (Kontostand als Dezimalzahl).
Formatted with 2 decimal places.

Currency.description:
Currency code in ISO 4217 format (Währungscode).
Common values: EUR (Euro), CHF (Swiss Franc)
